title: Podman `build --pull` vs docker behavior
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**

/kind feature

**Description**

Podman `build --pull` right now behaves different compared to docker, which makes it hard to use as a drop-in replacement.

On docker, the `--pull` flags forces a re-pull of the image during build, whereas on podman it only specifies that the image has to be pulled if not present. I know that there is the `--pull-always` flag, but what is the intention of setting `--pull=false` on build? From my understanding this will fail always if the image is not available locally, right?

comments.body:
@saschagrunert  this is a partial WIP  https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1873 on the Buildah side.   In short `podman --pull=false` fails currently if the image is not present locally.  It will not pull from the repo.  `docker --pull=false` tries to use the local image and then pulls from the repo if it's not present.  I've fixed that part of the equation in 1873 and added a `--pull-never` functionality to do the current  '--pull=false` functionality.  That is use local, and fail if not present (don't pull).

While going through that I also noticed that `podman --pull=true' and 'docker - -pull=true' were not in sync.   I'm trying to figure that one out now.

> While going through that I also noticed that `podman --pull=true' and 'docker - -pull=true' were not in sync. I'm trying to figure that one out now.

Cool, thanks! I think it would be fine to have a more fine-granular option set for buildah, while podman sticks to the docker behavior, right? I don't see a big reason for having both, `--pull` and `--pull-always` in podman.

I think the `podman --pull=true/false` should match `docker --pull=true/false` functionality.  However, I think it's good the have the --pull-always and --pull-never functionality in both Podman and Buildah.  If folks want it they can use it, if not, it's easy to ignore.
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.

@saschagrunert beat me to the punch as usual, but I want to leave a few breadcrumbs here.  https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/4506 merged in Buildah 1.11.5 which contains https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1873 that fixes this, we now match Docker's pull functionality and have a litle bit extra in --pull-always and --pull-never
